line: # This hand-written corpus exercises the complete vendored INI grammar.
; This grammar also recognizes hash and semicolon comments after value text.
# Spaces before a hash comment receive the leading-whitespace scope.
; A tab before a semicolon comment does too.
; A semicolon comment with path C:\temp and emoji 📝.
# Four leading spaces are captured separately.
; Two leading tabs are captured separately.
value = text # this hash begins an inline comment in the vendored grammar
other = text ; this semicolon also begins an inline comment
color = #7f5af0 is likewise treated as a comment
quoted_markers = '# not a comment; still a string'
after = comments do not consume the next line
japanese = '設定を読み込みました'
description = 'Unicode outside the BMP tests UTF-16 offsets'
space around key = only the final word before equals is the definition
colon: is not an equals separator
.leading = the leading dot is outside the captured key
-leading = the leading hyphen is outside the captured key
trailing. = a trailing dot prevents a key match
trailing- = a trailing hyphen prevents a key match
[first] trailing text is outside the section scope
[first]second] the section scope stops at the first closing bracket
single_note = 'The grammar permits a single-quoted string
ruleStack: [(1, source.ini, source.ini),(16, source.ini string.quoted.single.ini, source.ini string.quoted.single.ini)]

line: to continue across physical lines, including = and [brackets],
ruleStack: [(1, source.ini, source.ini),(16, source.ini string.quoted.single.ini, source.ini string.quoted.single.ini)]

line: until an unescaped closing quote appears here.'
double_note = "The double-quoted rule can likewise continue
ruleStack: [(1, source.ini, source.ini),(20, source.ini string.quoted.double.ini, source.ini string.quoted.double.ini)]

line: over a newline and preserve non-ASCII text such as Zürich,
ruleStack: [(1, source.ini, source.ini),(20, source.ini string.quoted.double.ini, source.ini string.quoted.double.ini)]

line: ending when the next double quote appears."
unicode_escape_text = 'literal\u263A is scoped as one escape pair'
double_has_no_escape_rule = "backslashes \n \t stay in the string scope"
; Closing comments verify that multiline string state was balanced.
# Indented hash comment after every specialty.
